description: Christoph Schlingensief (24 October 1960, Oberhausen – 21 August 2010, Berlin) was a German film and theatre director, actor, artist, and author. Initially working as an independent underground filmmaker, Schlingensief later began staging productions for renowned theatres and festivals, which often were accompanied by public controversies. In the final years before his death, he also worked for opera houses, and established himself as an artist. This is his fluxes oratorio "Church of Fear and the Alien within me", which received the Golden Lion Award at the Biennale 2011 in Venice.
